summary: Returns data for specified metric based on provided params
description: Returns data for specified metric based on provided params. Endpoint: GET /c/{company_id}/report/detail. Required parameters: metric, company_id. Optional parameters include filters, limit, interval, date_start, date_end, date_field, end_point, primary_group, and more.

params.name: filters
params.description: Details for specific metrics might require additional filters: ‘interval’ (formatted as ‘YYYY-MM’ or ‘YYYY-MM-DD’, applicable for: applications_over_time, candidates_over_time, disqualifications_over_time, evaluations_over_time, events_over_time, hires_over_time, jobs_activity_over_time, pipeline_events_over_time, views_over_time, visits_over_time metrics); ‘segment’ filter (one of: entered, disqualified, applicable for dropoff_rate metric), ‘segment’ filter (one of entered, proceeded, applicable for proceed_rate metric).

params.name: limit
params.description: Limit number of results. Defaults to 30.

params.name: interval
params.description: Interval used to group results. Can be: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly. Required for trend metrics.

params.name: date_start
params.description: Used when date_range is ‘range’. Date from which data should be fetched. Should be in format ‘2022-03-01’.

params.name: date_end
params.description: Used when date_range is ‘range’. Date to which data should be fetched. Should be in format ‘2022-03-01’.

params.name: date_field
params.description: Field used for filtering by date.

params.name: end_point
params.description: Used only for ‘custom_time_based’ metric. Possible values are limited based on provided ‘start_point’ parameter. Can be: candidate_disqualified, candidate_hired, candidate_start_date, job_closed, job_created, job_filled, job_published, requisition_approved, requisition_filled, requisition_sent_for_approval.

params.name: primary_group
params.description: Attribute used to aggregate results. Required for funnel metrics.

params.name: start_point
params.description: Used only for ‘custom_time_based’ metric’. Can be: candidate_applied, candidate_hired, job_created, job_published, requisition_approved, requisition_created, requisition_sent_for_approval.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number used for pagination, can be used only with the limit option. Defaults to 1.

params.name: unit_precision
params.description: Attribute used to adjust precision of time calculations. Can be: seconds, minutes. Note: minutes option is deprecated - please use seconds.

params.name: date_resource
params.description: Resource used for filtering by date.

params.name: date_range
params.description: Date filter. Can be: range, today, yesterday, this_week, last_week, this_month, last_month, this_quarter, last_quarter, this_year, last_year, last_7_days, last_14_days, last_30_days, last_60_days, last_90_days, last_365_days. If value is range, additional parameters ‘date_start’ and ‘date_end’ must be provided.

example_request:
GET /c/{company_id}/report/detail

Required params: metric, company_id

Optional params: filters, limit, interval, date_start, date_end, date_field, end_point, primary_group
